assumptions: The recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a monetary item denominated in a foreign currency.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation because the relevant group entities have different functional currencies.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup foreign-currency receivable is relevant to analyse under the fair value hedge model because IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the resulting exchange differences affect profit or loss.
